top_categories.workout_context: 61ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Noosa Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Noosa Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 99, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Noosa Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
6,070ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Noosa Classic's paved roads through the rolling Noosa Hinterland forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 99 miles.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 99 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
6,070ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 99 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,070ft of climbing on paved roads through the rolling Noosa Hinterland does to your power file on race day.
Tempo is Noosa Classic's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 99 miles on paved roads through the rolling Noosa Hinterland. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Noosa Classic’s 99 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and G Spot to prepare you for 99 miles of paved roads through the rolling noosa hinterland and mary valley in Tewantin, Noosa, Queensland, Australia.
